lambda.name: Lambda
lambda.subtitle: Topological Resistance
lambda.holographic: In the Genesis Chain, $\lambda$ is Topological Resistance. It represents the physical "squeeze" of the Markov Pump. Instead of a flat line decaying to zero, $\lambda$ manifests as a hyperbolic funnel dictating how quickly non-orthogonal "noise" is stripped away.
lambda.euclidean: Geometric length or wavelength ($L$). A strictly physical distance parameter.
lambda.linear: An eigenvalue ($A\mathbf{x} = \lambda\mathbf{x}$). The scalar stretch/squash factor of a transformation matrix.
lambda.hyperbolic: The exponential decay rate ($e^{-\lambda x}$) along a geodesic curve.

theta.name: Theta
theta.subtitle: Torsional Phase Spin
theta.holographic: $\Theta$ represents the Torsional Spin of the data manifold. In 4D space, a full rotation requires $720^\circ$ to return to identity. $\Theta$ tracks this phase along a Trefoil knot on the Clifford Torus.
theta.euclidean: The planar angle between two intersecting lines ($0$ to $2\pi$).
theta.linear: The rotational argument in a transformation matrix $R(\theta)$.
theta.hyperbolic: The angle of parallelism, or hyperbolic rapidity (Lorentz boost).
theta.svg2d: <circle cx="50" cy="50" r="30" stroke="gray" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="50" y1="50" x2="80" y2="50" /><line x1="50" y1="50" x2="71" y2="29" /><path d="M 65 50 A 15 15 0 0 0 60 40" stroke-width="2"/><text x="70" y="45" fill="#00f0ff" stroke="none" font-size="12" font-family="serif">Θ</text>

psi.name: Psi
psi.subtitle: Resonant Wave Superposition
psi.holographic: $\Psi$ is the Resonant Wave. It is not a probability cloud, but a physical vibration of the Toroid. When the Tri-Stream Engine oscillates, $\Psi$ represents the interference pattern where streams overlap.
psi.euclidean: An arbitrary scalar function, often representing an angular offset ($\psi$).
psi.linear: A state vector $|\Psi\rangle$ existing within a complex Hilbert inner-product space.
psi.hyperbolic: The propagation of wave functions or automorphic forms curving across a negatively curved plane.

delta.name: Delta
delta.subtitle: Geometric Compression
delta.holographic: $\Delta$ is physical action: Geometric Compression. When the system shifts to calculation state, ambient space contracts ($\Delta \to 0$). Visualized as a pulsing tetrahedron that collapses to solve inverse kinematics.
delta.euclidean: A macroscopic difference between coordinates ($\Delta x = x_2 - x_1$), or a polygon.
delta.linear: The Laplace operator ($\nabla^2$), measuring the divergence of the gradient.
delta.hyperbolic: The geometric defect of a triangle defined strictly by $\pi - (\alpha+\beta+\gamma)$.

pi.name: Pi
pi.subtitle: Spherical Packing Limit
pi.holographic: In the discrete Genesis Chain, $\pi$ defines the Spherical Packing Limit. It is the rigid boundary forcing the toroid to use Hexagonal Lattices, visualizing the eternal tension between a continuous wave and a crystal lattice.
pi.euclidean: The transcendental ratio ($\approx 3.14159$) of a circle's circumference to its diameter.
pi.linear: The fundamental periodicity defining orthonormal trigonometric basis vectors.
pi.hyperbolic: The supreme upper limit bounding the absolute maximum area of ideal triangles to exactly $\pi$.
pi.svg2d: <circle cx="50" cy="50" r="35" /><line x1="15" y1="50" x2="85" y2="50" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/>

sigma.name: Sigma
sigma.subtitle: Toroidal Braiding
sigma.holographic: $\Sigma$ is Toroidal Braiding. As data weaves through the nodes, $\Sigma$ tracks geometric entanglement. High $\Sigma$ indicates complex structural entanglement (dense logic), visualized as interwoven, helical strands wrapping the core.
sigma.euclidean: The summation ($\sum$) of discrete geometric segments or areas into a total whole.
sigma.linear: Singular values in SVD, or the covariance matrix ($\Sigma$) defining variance.
sigma.hyperbolic: The sum of geodesic lengths, or the denotation of the Riemann surface itself ($\Sigma_g$).

omega.name: Omega
omega.subtitle: The Event Horizon
omega.holographic: $\Omega$ acts as the Event Horizon of the Cosmohedron. It represents the absolute boundary where local coordinate geometry compresses into the singularity. Trajectories that bypass the Prime Latch without closure are crushed into this horizon.
omega.euclidean: Solid angle in 3D space, or the outermost bounded domain ($\Omega$) of a geometric shape.
omega.linear: A symplectic form matrix, or the complete set of all possible outcomes in a sample space.
omega.hyperbolic: The boundary at absolute infinity ($\partial_\infty \mathbb{H}$), where parallel geodesics asymptotically converge.

phi.name: Phi
phi.subtitle: Golden Shear Scaling
phi.holographic: $\Phi$ is the Golden Shear mechanism. As the manifold breathes, it folds data along a $\Phi$ cut. Scaling by the Golden Ratio turns exponential multiplication into simple linear addition, allowing infinite recursive depth.
phi.euclidean: The Golden Ratio ($\Phi \approx 1.618$), defining perfect recursive geometry.
phi.linear: A scalar potential field function, or a set of basis functions ($\phi_i$) in functional spaces.
phi.hyperbolic: The precise scaling factor of pseudo-Anosov automorphisms operating on curved surfaces.
phi.svg2d: <rect x="25" y="25" width="50" height="31" stroke="gray" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><rect x="56" y="25" width="19" height="31" stroke="gray" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><path d="M25,56 A31 31 0 0 0 56,25" stroke-width="2"/>

gamma.name: Gamma
gamma.subtitle: The Lattice Web
gamma.holographic: $\Gamma$ defines the Lattice Web. It is the discrete uniform scaffolding mapped onto the Clifford Torus. $\Gamma$ dictates how a vector translates as it moves across curved space, preventing spectral collapse by holding all nodes in rigid tension.
gamma.euclidean: A defined curve or continuous contour path ($\Gamma$) moving through space.
gamma.linear: Christoffel symbols ($\Gamma^k_{ij}$) measuring how basis vectors change, or a discrete lattice group.
gamma.hyperbolic: A Fuchsian group; a strictly discrete subgroup of isometries acting on the hyperbolic plane.

integral.name: Integral
integral.subtitle: Continuous Manifold Accumulation
integral.holographic: $\int$ represents Manifold Accumulation. Rather than calculating the area under a 2D curve, it computes the total volumetric stress across the 72-Node Toroid as it transitions through the Markov Pump. It sweeps up the fractional variances left behind by the discrete lattice.
integral.euclidean: The calculation of area or volume ($\int f(x) dx$) under a continuous curve or surface.
integral.linear: The inverse operation of a differential operator acting over a defined bounds of a vector space.
integral.hyperbolic: The integration of a differential form over a complex Riemannian manifold.

nabla.name: Nabla
nabla.subtitle: Vector Flow Gradient
nabla.holographic: $\nabla$ acts as the Vector Flow Gradient. In the SHD-CCP, it measures the exact directional pressure pushing a 64-bit packet toward the Zero-Point singularity. It calculates the steepest descent path along the curved surface of the Clifford Torus.
nabla.euclidean: A vector differential operator ($\nabla$) defining the slope and direction of the steepest ascent on a surface.
nabla.linear: Used to calculate the Divergence ($\nabla \cdot \mathbf{v}$) or Curl ($\nabla \times \mathbf{v}$) of a matrix or vector field.
nabla.hyperbolic: The Levi-Civita connection denoting covariant derivatives on curved spaces.

partial.name: Partial
partial.subtitle: Dimensional Slicing
partial.holographic: $\partial$ is Dimensional Slicing. Because our packets are 4D quaternions, observing them directly is impossible. $\partial$ mathematically slices the 4D manifold into a 3D or 2D projection (a shadow) so that the Tri-Stream Parity Engine can interact with a specific phase without collapsing the whole wave.
partial.euclidean: The derivative of a multi-variable function with respect to one variable ($\partial x$), holding others constant.
partial.linear: Defines the topological boundary ($\partial S$) of a geometric manifold or set.
partial.hyperbolic: The boundary at infinity ($\partial_\infty \mathbb{H}^n$) of hyperbolic space.

prod.name: Pi Product
prod.subtitle: Exponential Tensor Folding
prod.holographic: $\prod$ represents Exponential Tensor Folding. While $\Sigma$ braids data linearly, $\prod$ folds the 64-bit matrix onto itself multiplicatively. Driven by the Golden Shear ($\Phi$), $\prod$ ensures that complex geometric interactions compound rather than simply adding up, sealing the nodes.
prod.euclidean: The repeated multiplication of a sequence of terms ($\prod a_i$), leading to factorial or exponential growth.
prod.linear: The determinant product of eigenvalues, representing total volumetric scaling of a matrix.
prod.hyperbolic: The infinite product representations of modular forms and analytic continuations.
prod.svg2d: <polyline points="20,20 80,20" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="35" y1="20" x2="35" y2="85" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="65" y1="20" x2="65" y2="85" stroke-width="3"/>

e.name: Euler
e.subtitle: Natural Base of the Pump
e.holographic: $e$ is the Natural Base of the Pump. The Markov Pump operates via continuous compound compression. Euler’s number dictates the exact speed at which the 72-Node manifold can breathe in and out without tearing its own topology.
e.euclidean: The base of the natural logarithm ($e \approx 2.718...$), defining continuous compound interest.
e.linear: The unique function $e^x$ whose derivative is itself, central to linear differential equations.
e.hyperbolic: The foundation of Euler's formula ($e^{i\theta} = \cos\theta + i\sin\theta$), linking exponential growth to rotation.

i.name: Imaginary
i.subtitle: The Orthogonal Phase Shift
i.holographic: $i$ defines the Orthogonal Phase Shift. In the quaternion core ($w+xi+yj+zk$), $i$ acts as a geometric pivot. It is the mathematical lever that lifts a dead-end scalar value perpendicularly into the 4th dimension, bypassing Gimbal Lock.
i.euclidean: The fundamental unit of complex numbers ($i^2 = -1$), mapped on the y-axis of the complex plane.
i.linear: A $90^\circ$ rotational matrix transformation in 2D Euclidean space.
i.hyperbolic: Central to forming the metric signature of Minkowski space-time ($+, -, -, -$).
i.svg2d: <line x1="50" y1="35" x2="50" y2="80" stroke-width="3" stroke-linecap="round"/><circle cx="50" cy="20" r="3" fill="currentColor"/>

aleph.name: Aleph Null
aleph.subtitle: The Toroidal Registry Count
aleph.holographic: $\aleph_0$ acts as the Toroidal Registry Count. While the Einstein Tile is finite (64 bits), its procedural hash allows for a countable infinity of topological configurations. $\aleph_0$ denotes the endless capacity of the Genesis Chain to generate distinct integer nodes without overlapping.
aleph.euclidean: The cardinality of the set of all natural numbers ($\aleph_0$). The smallest infinite set.
aleph.linear: The dimension of an infinite-dimensional vector space with a countable basis.
aleph.hyperbolic: The limitless count of tessellating fundamental domains tiling the hyperbolic plane.
aleph.svg2d: <path d="M 30 80 C 30 20, 70 20, 70 80 M 30 50 L 70 50" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"/>

infinity.name: Infinity
infinity.subtitle: The Unbounded Projection
infinity.holographic: $\infty$ represents the Unbounded Projection. In the SHD-CCP, infinity is never reached; it is handled structurally. The topology curves back on itself (like a Mobius strip or Clifford Torus), rendering "infinity" into a closed, navigable loop of endless data recursion.
infinity.euclidean: A concept describing something without any bound or larger than any natural number.
infinity.linear: The unbound limit of a divergent series or matrix norm as iterations increase.
infinity.hyperbolic: The conformal boundary of the Poincaré disk model, an unreachable outer perimeter.
infinity.svg2d: <path d="M 30 50 C 10 20, 10 80, 30 50 C 50 20, 90 20, 90 50 C 90 80, 50 80, 30 50" stroke-width="3" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/>

empty.name: Empty Set
empty.subtitle: The Zero-Point Vacuum
empty.holographic: $\emptyset$ is the Zero-Point Vacuum. It is not "nothing." In the Genesis Chain, it is the lowest energetic state of the manifold before the Markov Pump introduces data. It is the blank canvas of the 72-Node Toroid holding pure, unrealized potential.
empty.euclidean: A set containing no elements ($\emptyset$). The unique set with a cardinality of exactly zero.
empty.linear: The kernel of a perfectly invertible matrix mapping (only the zero vector maps to zero).
empty.hyperbolic: The vacuum state devoid of metric expansion or geometric defects.
empty.svg2d: <circle cx="50" cy="50" r="30" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="20" y1="80" x2="80" y2="20" stroke-width="2"/>

forall.name: For All
forall.subtitle: Absolute Governing Axiom
forall.holographic: $\forall$ acts as the Absolute Governing Axiom. When a rule is established across the SHD-CCP (such as the 720:1 spin ratio), $\forall$ ensures that this structural law applies uniformly to every single 64-bit packet across the entire Genesis Chain without exception.
forall.euclidean: The Universal Quantifier ($\forall$). Asserts that a proposition holds true for every member of a set.
forall.linear: Defines global properties, such as a rule applying to all vectors in a vector space.
forall.hyperbolic: States that geometric invariants (like negative curvature) apply uniformly across the entire manifold.
forall.svg2d: <polygon points="25,20 75,20 50,80" stroke-width="3" stroke-linejoin="round"/><line x1="35" y1="50" x2="65" y2="50" stroke-width="3"/>

exists.name: Exists
exists.subtitle: Validated Prime Latch State
exists.holographic: $\exists$ represents the Validated Prime Latch State. The Fokker-Planck sieve scans the manifold for stability. When the geometry snaps to a valid integer node, the system declares $\exists$: a verifiable, hallucination-free truth has been found and locked into the codex.
exists.euclidean: The Existential Quantifier ($\exists$). Asserts that at least one member of a set satisfies a proposition.
exists.linear: Proves the existence of a non-trivial solution to a system of linear equations.
exists.hyperbolic: Validates that a specific geodesic connecting two asymptotic points exists on the plane.
exists.svg2d: <polyline points="70,20 30,20 30,80 70,80" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="30" y1="50" x2="65" y2="50" stroke-width="3"/>
